bug_data.description:
Crash report: https://crash-stats.mozilla.org/report/index/46b917b7-ef6a-4040-aecf-c93c60251003

MOZ_CRASH Reason:
```
MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT(NS_IsMainThread()) (nsXPCWrappedJS::AddRef called off main thread)
```

Top 10 frames:
```
0  xul.dll  nsXPCWrappedJS::AddRef()  js/xpconnect/src/XPCWrappedJS.cpp:253
0  xul.dll  nsXPTCStubBase::AddRef()  xpcom/reflect/xptcall/xptcall.cpp:24
1  xul.dll  mozilla::RefPtrTraits<nsIStreamListener>::AddRef(nsIStreamListener*)  mfbt/RefPtr.h:48
1  xul.dll  nsCOMPtr<nsIStreamListener>::nsCOMPtr(nsCOMPtr<nsIStreamListener> const&)  xpcom/base/nsCOMPtr.h:387
1  xul.dll  nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished(nsresult)  uriloader/base/nsURILoader.cpp:238
2  xul.dll  mozilla::net::InterceptFailedOnStop::OnDataFinished(nsresult)  netwerk/protocol/http/HttpBaseChannel.cpp:1434
3  xul.dll  mozilla::net::nsHTTPCompressConv::OnDataFinished(nsresult)  netwerk/streamconv/converters/nsHTTPCompressConv.cpp:1016
4  xul.dll  mozilla::net::HttpChannelChild::SendOnDataFinished(nsresult const&)  netwerk/protocol/http/HttpChannelChild.cpp:858
4  xul.dll  mozilla::net::HttpChannelChild::ProcessOnStopRequest::<lambda_30>::operator()...  netwerk/protocol/http/HttpChannelChild.cpp:930
4  xul.dll  std::invoke(mozilla::net::HttpChannelChild::ProcessOnStopRequest::<lambda_30>&)  /builds/worker/fetches/vs/VC/Tools/MSVC/14.39.33519/include/type_traits:1729
```

I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

comments.text: Thoughts on Valentin's question in comment 0 about QI to COMPtr for a JS object OffMainThread?
comments.raw_text: Thoughts on Valentin's question in comment 0 about QI to COMPtr for a JS object OffMainThread?
comments.creator: rjesup@jesup.org
comments.author: rjesup@jesup.org
valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Valentin Gosu [:valentin] (he/him) from comment #0)
> I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

If we can't do that, then we should QI to nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener in OnStartRequest calling CheckListenerChain and keep a reference around (and maybe figure out a way to clear it at the same time as the listener)
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.text:
(In reply to Valentin Gosu [:valentin] (he/him) from comment #0)
> I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

If we can't do that, then we should QI to nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener in OnStartRequest calling CheckListenerChain and keep a reference around (and maybe figure out a way to clear it at the same time as the listener)

comments.creator: nika@thelayzells.com
comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Valentin Gosu [:valentin] (he/him) from comment #0)
> I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

It's quite unsafe to QI a COMPtr to a JS object from off-main-thread, yeah. That can run arbitrary JS.
comments.text:
(In reply to Valentin Gosu [:valentin] (he/him) from comment #0)
> I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

It's quite unsafe to QI a COMPtr to a JS object from off-main-thread, yeah. That can run arbitrary JS.
comments.author: nika@thelayzells.com
valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text: The problem here seems to be with nsIStreamConverter which extends nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener but can be JS implemented by [converter-child.js](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/devtools/client/jsonview/converter-child.js#49) and [PdfStreamConverter](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/toolkit/components/pdfjs/content/PdfStreamConverter.sys.mjs#926,930)
comments.text: The problem here seems to be with nsIStreamConverter which extends nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener but can be JS implemented by [converter-child.js](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/devtools/client/jsonview/converter-child.js#49) and [PdfStreamConverter](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/toolkit/components/pdfjs/content/PdfStreamConverter.sys.mjs#926,930)

comments.author: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
I've been struggling to create a testcase for this.
My findings so far:
- Crashes on HTML5 thread
- So the retargetting in [nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/parser/html/nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp#1216) is successful.
- I think the call to CheckListenerChain initially succeeds, but then nsURILoader::OpenChannel happens to add a listener to the chain - in this case the pdfjs converter. Not sure where that is coming from, but I suspect it may be an object or embed - since it's driven by the HTML parser.
- By the time we call onDataFinished, the pdfjs listener will be addreffed off main thread when QIing, which leads to the crash.
- I see nsHTTPCompressConv is also on the stack. I initially thought this may be caused by [mozilla::net::nsHTTPCompressConv::MaybeRetarget](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/netwerk/streamconv/converters/nsHTTPCompressConv.cpp#287), but since it's on the HTML5 thread we don't retarget again here.
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.text:
I've been struggling to create a testcase for this.
My findings so far:
- Crashes on HTML5 thread
- So the retargetting in [nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/parser/html/nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp#1216) is successful.
- I think the call to CheckListenerChain initially succeeds, but then nsURILoader::OpenChannel happens to add a listener to the chain - in this case the pdfjs converter. Not sure where that is coming from, but I suspect it may be an object or embed - since it's driven by the HTML parser.
- By the time we call onDataFinished, the pdfjs listener will be addreffed off main thread when QIing, which leads to the crash.
- I see nsHTTPCompressConv is also on the stack. I initially thought this may be caused by [mozilla::net::nsHTTPCompressConv::MaybeRetarget](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/netwerk/streamconv/converters/nsHTTPCompressConv.cpp#287), but since it's on the HTML5 thread we don't retarget again here.

comments.creator: csabou@mozilla.com
comments.author: csabou@mozilla.com

comments.raw_text:
Since nsHTTPCompressConv::CheckListenerChain will return NS_OK in the
content process, that means OnDataFinished might be called even if
not all the listeners in the listener chain support being called
off-main-thread. This is the case for a JS implemented listener.
It is important to not QI a JS implemented listener off main thread.
Since the listener wouldn't benefit from OnDataFinished since it's
limited to the main thread, that means we can avoid calling it
entirely.
comments.author: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.text:
Created attachment 9529607
Bug 1992512 - nsHTTPCompressConv::OnDataFinished should not QI listener off-main-thread r=#necko


Since nsHTTPCompressConv::CheckListenerChain will return NS_OK in the
content process, that means OnDataFinished might be called even if
not all the listeners in the listener chain support being called
off-main-thread. This is the case for a JS implemented listener.
It is important to not QI a JS implemented listener off main thread.
Since the listener wouldn't benefit from OnDataFinished since it's
limited to the main thread, that means we can avoid calling it
entirely.
:valentin what are the next steps here? Do we just wait for the 146 uptake to analyze crashdata?
comments.author: dsmith@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text: :valentin what are the next steps here? Do we just wait for the 146 uptake to analyze crashdata?

comments.raw_text:

MOZ_CRASH Reason:
```
MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT(NS_IsMainThread()) (nsXPCWrappedJS::AddRef called off main thread)
```

I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

comments.text:
(In reply to Valentin Gosu [:valentin] (he/him) from comment #0)
> I'm not sure if this can be fixed by just making [nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dcdbc805276cd5bf131cac2dbf53dda1538a32d5/netwerk/base/nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener.idl#16) a builtinclass, or whether we can never QI a COMPtr to a JS object off main thread?

It's quite unsafe to QI a COMPtr to a JS object from off-main-thread, yeah. That can run arbitrary JS.

comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text: The problem here seems to be with nsIStreamConverter which extends nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener but can be JS implemented by [converter-child.js](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/devtools/client/jsonview/converter-child.js#49) and [PdfStreamConverter](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/toolkit/components/pdfjs/content/PdfStreamConverter.sys.mjs#926,930)
comments.text: The problem here seems to be with nsIStreamConverter which extends nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener but can be JS implemented by [converter-child.js](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/devtools/client/jsonview/converter-child.js#49) and [PdfStreamConverter](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/toolkit/components/pdfjs/content/PdfStreamConverter.sys.mjs#926,930)

valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
I've been struggling to create a testcase for this.
My findings so far:
- Crashes on HTML5 thread
- So the retargetting in [nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/parser/html/nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp#1216) is successful.
- I think the call to CheckListenerChain initially succeeds, but then nsURILoader::OpenChannel happens to add a listener to the chain - in this case the pdfjs converter. Not sure where that is coming from, but I suspect it may be an object or embed - since it's driven by the HTML parser.
- By the time we call onDataFinished, the pdfjs listener will be addreffed off main thread when QIing, which leads to the crash.
- I see nsHTTPCompressConv is also on the stack. I initially thought this may be caused by [mozilla::net::nsHTTPCompressConv::MaybeRetarget](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/netwerk/streamconv/converters/nsHTTPCompressConv.cpp#287), but since it's on the HTML5 thread we don't retarget again here.

I've been struggling to create a testcase for this.
My findings so far:
- Crashes on HTML5 thread
- So the retargetting in [nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/parser/html/nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp#1216) is successful.
- I think the call to CheckListenerChain initially succeeds, but then nsURILoader::OpenChannel happens to add a listener to the chain - in this case the pdfjs converter. Not sure where that is coming from, but I suspect it may be an object or embed - since it's driven by the HTML parser.
- By the time we call onDataFinished, the pdfjs listener will be addreffed off main thread when QIing, which leads to the crash.
- I see nsHTTPCompressConv is also on the stack. I initially thought this may be caused by [mozilla::net::nsHTTPCompressConv::MaybeRetarget](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/91e6069b9c1e7289d0c727b5110e144edc589204/netwerk/streamconv/converters/nsHTTPCompressConv.cpp#287), but since it's on the HTML5 thread we don't retarget again here.

comments.text: https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/rev/177b052bff79

Created attachment 9529607
Bug 1992512 - nsHTTPCompressConv::OnDataFinished should not QI listener off-main-thread r=#necko


Since nsHTTPCompressConv::CheckListenerChain will return NS_OK in the
content process, that means OnDataFinished might be called even if
not all the listeners in the listener chain support being called
off-main-thread. This is the case for a JS implemented listener.
It is important to not QI a JS implemented listener off main thread.
Since the listener wouldn't benefit from OnDataFinished since it's
limited to the main thread, that means we can avoid calling it
entirely.

crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: Off-main-thread call to nsXPCWrappedJS::AddRef when QI (QueryInterface) is performed on a JS listener, violating the assertion that AddRef must be called on the main thread.
symptoms: Crash occurs when calling nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished(nsresult) involving JS stream listeners off the main thread
reproduction_steps: Trigger network loading of a resource involving a JS-implemented nsIStreamConverter (e.g., pdfjs or JSONView converters)
Cause the stream listener chain to be retargeted off-main-thread
Allow nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished to QI a COMPtr to a JS listener off the main thread
bug_analysis.technical_details: The crash occurs due to a MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT failure asserting that nsXPCWrappedJS::AddRef must only be called on the main thread. The bug arises when an off-main-thread component attempts to QueryInterface (QI) a COMPtr to a JS-implemented nsIStreamListener, specifically one that extends nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener. The listener chain includes JS-implemented converters like pdfjs's PdfStreamConverter or devtools' converter-child.js. The retargeting logic in nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest is successful, but subsequent additions to the listener chain by nsURILoader cause unsafe off-main-thread QI calls. Fix involved validating retargetable listeners after off-main-thread retargeting succeeded.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Intermittent crashes during page loads or resource handling involving JS-based stream converters, potentially resulting in browser instability or termination.

common_patterns: Crashes occurring during event dispatch or listener invocation
Crashes involving off-main-thread operations with JS objects
Use-after-free or invalid QI (QueryInterface) calls on JS listeners or wrapped JS objects
potential_root_causes: Race conditions or thread-safety issues when accessing JS listener objects off the main thread
Improper lifetime management of XPCWrappedJS objects leading to premature release
Invalid or unsafe QI calls on JS objects that are no longer valid on non-main threads
relevant_fix_clues: Fixes likely involved ensuring that JS listeners are only accessed on the main thread
Improvements in refcounting and lifetime management of nsXPCWrappedJS objects
Avoiding QI calls on JS listeners off the main thread by introducing proper thread checks or dispatches
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the root cause is related to unsafe access and lifetime management of JS listener objects off the main thread, particularly involving nsXPCWrappedJS AddRef and QueryInterface calls. The crashes seem to stem from race conditions or invalid thread usage, and resolving these issues typically requires enforcing main-thread-only access and improving the refcounting and lifetime logic for XPCWrappedJS objects.

Improvements in refcounting and lifetime management of nsXPCWrappedJS objects
Avoiding QI calls on JS listeners off the main thread by introducing proper thread checks or dispatches
similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the root cause is related to unsafe access and lifetime management of JS listener objects off the main thread, particularly involving nsXPCWrappedJS AddRef and QueryInterface calls. The crashes seem to stem from race conditions or invalid thread usage, and resolving these issues typically requires enforcing main-thread-only access and improving the refcounting and lifetime logic for XPCWrappedJS objects.

refined_analysis.root_cause: Off-main-thread QueryInterface and AddRef calls on JS-implemented nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener objects violate XPConnect's main-thread-only assertions, causing MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT crashes in nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished.
symptoms: Crashes occur when JS stream listeners are accessed off the main thread during network stream completion.
reproduction_steps: Load a network resource using a JS-implemented nsIStreamConverter (such as pdfjs PdfStreamConverter).
nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest retargets nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener chain off the main thread.
nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished performs off-main-thread QueryInterface on JS listeners, triggering main-thread assertion failures.
refined_analysis.technical_details: The crash is caused by off-main-thread QI and AddRef calls on JS nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener objects. Although nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp correctly retargets listeners off the main thread, nsDocumentOpenInfo.cpp later invokes QI on these JS listeners off the main thread, violating XPConnect's main-thread-only rule. Fix requires enforcing main-thread affinity for QI/AddRef calls on JS listeners and validating listener thread targets after retargeting.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Intermittent browser crashes during page or resource loads involving JS-based stream converters reduce stability and cause termination.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: Add main-thread affinity checks in nsDocumentOpenInfo.cpp to prevent off-main-thread QI and AddRef on JS listeners. Enhance nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp to validate listener thread targets post-retargeting. Dispatch QI calls to the main thread or redesign listener updates to guarantee thread safety.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: Fix MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT crash in netwerk/protocol/http/nsDocumentOpenInfo.cpp (OnDataFinished) caused by off-main-thread QueryInterface and AddRef calls on JS nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener objects (js/xpconnect/src/XPCWrappedJS.cpp). The issue arises after parser/html/nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp retargets listeners (e.g., pdfjs stream converters) off the main thread, but nsDocumentOpenInfo.cpp performs QI calls off the main thread, violating XPConnect assertions. The fix enforces main-thread-only QI/AddRef on JS listeners and validates retargeting.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The analysis correctly identifies the root cause and affected components but is overly verbose and dense, which may reduce AI effectiveness. It lacks precise function references within key files (e.g., exact line or method details in nsDocumentOpenInfo.cpp and nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp). The reproduction steps are clear but could be more concise. The technical details are comprehensive but would benefit from a tighter focus on the critical thread-safety violation and exact call sites. The optimized_context should be significantly shortened and highlight specific functions and file paths to maximize AI-guided fix success.

filtered_context.concise_summary: Crash in nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished (netwerk/protocol/http/nsDocumentOpenInfo.cpp) due to off-main-thread QueryInterface and AddRef calls on JS-implemented nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener objects (js/xpconnect/src/XPCWrappedJS.cpp). Listeners retargeted off main thread by nsHtml5StreamParser::OnStartRequest (parser/html/nsHtml5StreamParser.cpp, e.g. pdfjs converters) cause MOZ_RELEASE_ASSERT(NS_IsMainThread()) failures. Fix requires enforcing main-thread-only QI/AddRef on JS listeners and validating retargeting to prevent off-main-thread XPConnect violations.
core_symptoms: Off-main-thread QI/AddRef on JS nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener causes XPConnect assertion failures
Crashes triggered during network resource loads using JS-based stream converters (e.g., pdfjs)
technical_constraints: XPConnect requires QI/AddRef on JS objects to happen on main thread
nsHtml5StreamParser retargets nsIThreadRetargetableStreamListener off main thread
nsDocumentOpenInfo::OnDataFinished violates this by calling QI/AddRef off main thread
Fix must enforce main-thread affinity for these calls and verify listener thread targets after retargeting
